model_summary: Initial gVAR-ready observation model for self-reported blunted positive affect, autopilot routines, outward 'fine' masking, guilt for not enjoying, general blunted affect, and reduced motivation. Design: 28 days, 5 momentary EMAs/day plus 1 morning diary/day. 6 criterion nodes (C01–C06) preserved from complaint; 8 predictor nodes (P01–P08) chosen to cover high-signal, modifiable behavioral and bio-social levers (behavioral activation, savoring/positive-event noticing, self-compassion, micro-activation energizers, mindful present-moment checks, social reconnection, sleep regularity, morning light). Sampling aligns with EMA schedule to support within-person gVAR modeling at 0–24h / 0–6h lags.
variable_selection_notes: Kept all 6 original criteria to preserve complaint fidelity. Selected 8 predictors from top-ranked PSYCHO/BIO solutions in signals (behavioral activation, savoring, self-compassion, micro-activations, mindful check-ins, social connection, sleep/circadian, morning light). Prioritized modifiable, EMA-measurable constructs with expected within-person variability at the chosen timescale. Total nodes = 14 (<=18).

momentary_ema.notes: Prompts semi-random within listed blocks to reduce predictability while covering daily context. Momentary variables sampled at every prompt (sampling_per_day = 5).

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary anchors sleep quality and morning light exposure (previous night / current morning).

passive_streams.notes: Optional passive streams: phone ambient light sensor aggregated 06:00-10:00 (minutes bright), daily step count (total). Use only with consent; included for triangulation with self-report morning light and activity.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Design provides dense intra-individual time series (5 prompts/day x 28 days) for momentary variables and daily diaries for sleep/light. Good candidate variables for gVAR; expect enough variability for pairwise estimation if adherence >= 60%.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 anchored Likert scales for self-report constructs to increase variance. Detrend weekly linear effects if observed; include time-of-day fixed effects in models to capture circadian patterns.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Risk concentrated on evening prompts and diary compliance. Suggest push reminders and brief items (one screen, 3–5 items per prompt). Consider planned missingness or multiple imputation for gVAR if <70% adherence.

safety_notes: This model is for measurement planning and not diagnostic. If responses indicate acute safety risk (suicidal ideation, self-harm intent), immediate clinical escalation is required — include an automated risk screen and local emergency instructions in the data-collection system.

expected_collinearity_risks: P01 and P04 (activation/adherence and micro-activation) may correlate at times; monitor variance inflation.
P02 and P01 may be correlated when activities produce savoring; consider testing for redundancy.
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C02 guilt may be zero-inflated if no relevant events are reported; include 'no event' metadata.
Passive P08 may have many zeros for indoor participants; include self-report fallback.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Use anchored 1–9 Likert scales as specified to maximize within-person variance.
Encourage completion windows and short items to reduce missingness.
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: High-complexity biomedical neuromodulation nodes (TMS, implantable) excluded due to low feasibility and out-of-scope for EMA gVAR.
Detailed nutrition microbiome measures deferred to later phases.
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 5 momentary prompts/day and 28 days, momentary variables will yield ~140 timepoints — adequate for pairwise gVAR if adherence >=60%. Daily diaries and passive aggregates complement temporal coverage. Recommended next steps: pilot for 1 week to assess adherence and within-person variance; pre-specify lag bins (0-6h, 0-24h, 24-48h) in modeling pipeline.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you feel guilty because you didn’t enjoy something you thought you should? Rate intensity. (1=None — 9=Very strong)
measurement.notes: If participant reports no relevant event, allow '0=no relevant event' option; treat as low intensity for time-series.

criteria.label: Behavioral autopilot / low moment-to-moment engagement
criteria.query_text_used: Behavioral autopilot / low moment-to-moment engagement | Operational item/signal: Since the last prompt, how often did you feel 'on autopilot' or not mentally present? (1=Not at all — 9=Almost always) | Polarity: lower_is_better | Notes: High-frequency capture important to detect micro-changes; can be complemented by passive activity/sedentary signals.

mapping.rationale: Variable maps to moment-to-moment engagement and autopilot (low present-moment awareness). The EMA item asks how often you felt 'on autopilot' or not mentally present, which directly targets present-moment awareness. Leaf 3475 describes present orientation / being in the moment / immediate awareness, aligning with the measurement signal and the intended construct (lower engagement corresponds to higher autopilot).

criteria.label: External presentation of being 'fine' despite internal disengagement
criteria.query_text_used: External presentation of being 'fine' despite internal disengagement | Operational item/signal: Since the last prompt, how much did you present as 'fine' outwardly while feeling different inside? (1=Not at all — 9=Very much) | Polarity: lower_is_better | Notes: Captures mismatch between outward functioning and inner state.

mapping.rationale: Variable label describes outward masking of affect (presenting as 'fine' while inner state is different). The most specific operational leaf among candidates is 'emotion regulation in displays' (outward emotional display regulation), which directly matches the mechanism of masking in EMA self-report. Measurement mode (EMA_self_report, mobile_app, momentary, 5 prompts/day, recall window since_last_prompt) aligns with capturing dynamic outward displays vs internal state. Pseudoprofile context supports blunted affect and masking behaviors, but we should map to the most specific construct available, i.e., regulation of emotional displays rather than broader internal affect concepts.

source_variable.label: Reduced motivation/interest to pursue non-routine pleasurable activities

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how motivated were you to start or plan an enjoyable non-routine activity? (1=None — 9=Very motivated)
measurement.notes: Designed to capture initiative changes across the day.

predictors.label: Behavioral activation: planned pleasant/activity adherence
predictors.query_text_used: Behavioral activation: planned pleasant/activity adherence | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Increasing engagement in planned pleasant or valued activities raises opportunity for positive reinforcement and restores reward sensitivity. | Intervention examples: Scheduling small pleasurable activities, graded activation, linking activities to values. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Since the last prompt, did you do a planned pleasant activity or take a step toward one? Rate adherence (1=No effort — 9=Fully carried out) | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies / Behavior_Change_and_Activation

mapping.rationale: The variable measures adherence to planned pleasant/valued activities (behavioral activation) via EMA. The closest leaf is behavioral_activation_scheduling (PSYCHO / change_process_and_therapeutic_phases / skill_acquisition / behavioral_planning_and_execution_skills / behavioral_activation_scheduling), which directly maps to scheduling and executing valued activities. This aligns with the measurement item (adherence to planned activity) and the proposed mechanism (increasing scheduled engagement boosts opportunities for positive reinforcement and activation, sustaining within-person variability).

source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Brief compassionate self-talk prompts, guided self-compassion exercises.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you use a self-compassion exercise or notice kinder self-talk? (1=None — 9=Used thoroughly)
measurement.notes: Directly measures use/intensity of self-compassion practice.

expected_effects.rationale: Self-compassion reduces guilt intensity within hours.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk; if high shame/guilt linked to self-harm, advise clinical referral.

The leaf 1387 sits under SOCIAL / social_support_and_belonging / belonging_isolation_and_meaning / loneliness_reduction_planning / micro_connection_opportunities_plan, explicitly addressing micro-level social opportunities to reduce isolation and improve belonging. This aligns with the measurement item (count of meaningful interactions + quality rating) by targeting the planning and availability of brief social contacts that could drive observed interactions and perceived quality. Mechanism: small, authentic social interactions lift mood, provide feedback, and reduce masking; end-of-day quality assessment reflects impact of these micro-connections. Pseudoprofile context favors modifiable, EMA-measurable social levers; 1387 is the most specific operational construct for brief interactions/outreach among candidates.

source_variable.label: Social connectedness: brief interactions / outreach
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Small social interactions can lift mood, provide meaningful feedback, and reduce masking by increasing authentic connection.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Short calls/texts, planned social check-ins, attending small gatherings.

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, how many meaningful social interactions did you have? (0,1-2,3+); and rate their quality 1-9
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: count + 1-9 quality rating (use quality as primary node)
measurement.notes: End-of-day summary to reduce burden; quality rating used for gVAR node.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: center; treat count separately as covariate if needed

expected_effects.rationale: High-quality interactions reduce internal masking and increase authentic affect over the day.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Ensure social prompts respect privacy and safety.

feasibility.notes: End-of-day reduces momentary burden; lower temporal resolution for gVAR but still informative.

predictors.label: Sleep quality and regularity (previous night)
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep quality and regularity (previous night) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Poor sleep and irregular circadian rhythm can increase autopilot, blunted affect, and reduced motivation. | Intervention examples: Bedtime consistency, sleep hygiene, light/dark exposure. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | How well did you sleep last night? (1=Very poorly — 9=Very well). Also capture sleep duration (hours). | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration / Sleep_Routines_and_Practices

mapping.rationale: Best match is idx=4548 as it directly targets 'sleep_quality_rating' within BIO. It encodes the variable Sleep quality and regularity as a self-assessment in daily retrospective logs (sleep_quality_rating) and aligns with measurement_type EMA_self_report via morning diary by asking 'How well did you sleep last night?' on a 1–9 scale (plus hours). This is the most specific operational construct among candidates for a morning-diary sleep-quality item, matching the variable label and mechanism that poor sleep and irregular circadian rhythms can increase autopilot, blunted affect, and reduce motivation. While duration is included in the variable notes, the selected leaf captures the core sleep quality signal most directly; duration could be captured via related diary entries if needed.

source_variable.label: Sleep quality and regularity (previous night)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Poor sleep and irregular circadian rhythm can increase autopilot, blunted affect, and reduced motivation.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Bedtime consistency, sleep hygiene, light/dark exposure.

measurement.item_or_signal: How well did you sleep last night? (1=Very poorly — 9=Very well). Also capture sleep duration (hours).
measurement.notes: Combine with passive step and light data for triangulation.

expected_effects.rationale: Better sleep reduces daytime autopilot and increases alertness the next day.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: If severe insomnia or hypersomnolence suspected, advise clinical evaluation.

feasibility.notes: Morning diary likely high-compliance if short.

predictors.label: Morning light exposure / early activity (minutes bright)
predictors.query_text_used: Morning light exposure / early activity (minutes bright) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Morning bright light and early activity support circadian entrainment, increasing daytime alertness and affective responsiveness. | Intervention examples: Morning outdoor exposure, light therapy, brief morning walks. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Minutes above threshold lux between 06:00-10:00 (daily aggregate) | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Neuromodulation_and_Neurostimulation / Light_and_Photo_Neuromodulation

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf is Morning Bright Light Exposure under BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / circadian_entrainment / light_cues / morning_bright_light_exposure. The variable represents morning light exposure (minutes above lux threshold), measured passively via ambient_light_sensor (06:00–10:00), aligning with the leaf's concept of morning bright light exposure and circadian entrainment through light cues. This is more specific than related leaves (e.g., outdoor_walk) and directly corresponds to the measurement item (minutes of bright light) and mechanism (circadian entrainment and alertness).

source_variable.label: Morning light exposure / early activity (minutes bright)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Morning bright light and early activity support circadian entrainment, increasing daytime alertness and affective responsiveness.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Morning outdoor exposure, light therapy, brief morning walks.

measurement.item_or_signal: Minutes above threshold lux between 06:00-10:00 (daily aggregate)
measurement.notes: Fallback to self-report morning diary if passive data unavailable: 'How much bright light exposure this morning? (1-9)'.

expected_effects.rationale: Morning light increases alertness and reduces autopilot across the day.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk; do not recommend bright light exposure for photosensitive conditions without medical advice.

feasibility.notes: Passive sensor may be missing for some phones; include self-report fallback.
